content.problemStatement:
You are given an undirected graph with nnn nodes and mmm edges. The nodes are numbered from 1 to nnn. Each edge eee of the graph has two integers associated with it, cost(cec_ece‚Äč) and value(vev_eve‚Äč). You are given three integers sss, ttt, and KKK.

The cost of a walk is the sum of costs of all the edges in the walk. Find the minimum cost of a walk from sss to ttt (s‚Č†ts \neq tsÓÄ†=t), such that the bitwise AND of values for all edges in the walk is at least KKK. If no such walk exists, print ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.

Note that the walk doesn't need to be a simple path. Any vertex or edge can come more than once in the walk you choose. If an edge eee comes ppp times, we add its cost ppp times to the cost of the walk.

Formally, a walk from sss to ttt is a sequence of vertices and edges u1,e1,u2,e2,‚čĮ‚ÄČ,ul‚ąí1,el‚ąí1,ulu_1, e_1, u_2, e_2, \cdots, u_{l-1}, e_{l-1}, u_{l}u1‚Äč,e1‚Äč,u2‚Äč,e2‚Äč,‚čĮ,ul‚ąí1‚Äč,el‚ąí1‚Äč,ul‚Äč, where eie_iei‚Äč is the edge between vertices uiu_{i}ui‚Äč and ui+1u_{i+1}ui+1‚Äč, u1=su_1 = su1‚Äč=s and ul=tu_l = tul‚Äč=t.

We need to minimize ‚ąĎi=1l‚ąí1cei\displaystyle \sum_{i=1}^{l-1} c_{e_i}i=1‚ąĎl‚ąí1‚Äčcei‚Äč‚Äč, while satisfying ve1&ve2&‚čĮ&vel‚ąí1‚Č•Kv_{e_1} \& v_{e_2} \& \cdots \& v_{e_{l-1}} \geq Kve1‚Äč‚Äč&ve2‚Äč‚Äč&‚čĮ&vel‚ąí1‚Äč‚Äč‚Č•K, where &\&& denotes the bitwise AND operator.


Input Format

First line will contain nnn and mmm
Each of the next mmm lines will contain the description of an edge in the graph. It will consist of 4 integers a,b,c,va, b, c, va,b,c,v denoting the endpoints, cost, and value of the edge respectively.
The last line contains s,t,Ks, t, Ks,t,K, as described in the statement.


Output Format
Print the minimum cost of a walk from sss to ttt, such that the bitwise AND of values for all edges in the walk is at least KKK. Print ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 if no such walk exists.
constraints: The graph contains no self-loops and multiedges.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Suppose we choose the edge between 1 and 3 and say that's our walk. Its cost is 2. But this is not a valid walk, because the AND of the edge values is 2. We need it to be ‚Č•\ge‚Č• 3.
So, instead suppose we choose the walk from 111 to 333 passing through 222. The bitwise AND is 3 & 7 = 3, and cost is 1 + 2 = 3. This is a valid walk, and you can check that you cannot reduce the cost any further. Hence 3 is the answer.
